message:
refactor(types): migrate SqliteValue::Text/Blob to Arc<str>/Arc<[u8]> for O(1) clone

Register copies (SCopy, Copy, ResultRow) are the hottest operation in
the VDBE engine. With owned String/Vec<u8>, every register-to-register
copy paid O(n) for heap allocation + memcpy. Switching to Arc<str> and
Arc<[u8]> makes these copies O(1) atomic refcount increments, which
dramatically reduces allocation pressure during full-table scans and
JOIN pipelines.

Changes in fsqlite-types:
- SqliteValue::Text now wraps Arc<str> instead of String
- SqliteValue::Blob now wraps Arc<[u8]> instead of Vec<u8>
- as_text()/as_blob() return slices directly from the Arc
- to_text() now calls .to_string() on the Arc<str>
- Display impl uses b.iter() for Arc<[u8]> iteration
- New From<Arc<str>> and From<Arc<[u8]>> impls for zero-cost construction
- Existing From<String>, From<&str>, From<Vec<u8>>, From<&[u8]> updated
  to convert through Arc
- decode_value/decode_value_into in record.rs simplified: Arc is
  immutable so buffer-reuse match arms are replaced with direct
  assignment (the O(1) clone benefit outweighs lost buffer reuse)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
